acceptance_criteria: Homepage uses a table-first layout; no left filter sidebar on the homepage; tabs are Top, Standard, and Blocktributes; rows include Bitmap plus the approved trait categories; no on-chain traits or floor 24h percent; recent sales module appears below the table; existing browse experience is available on /browse.
description: Replace the current parcel-browse homepage with a minimal market overview focused on Bitmap and blocktribute trait categories. The page should use a compact leaderboard layout with columns for Trait/Category, Floor, Volume, Sales, Listed, and Last 24h sparkline. Exclude floor change percentages and any on-chain leaderboard for the initial release. Keep the homepage focused on market summary, with the existing browse experience living on /browse.
title: Redesign homepage as minimalist trait market overview

acceptance_criteria: The homepage no longer renders the current browse grid; layout matches the approved wireframe; the table is the primary above-the-fold element; the design is minimal and data-first.
close_reason: Implemented homepage leaderboard shell and navigation
description: Implement the new landing page frame: compact header strip, BTC/USD toggle placeholder, See all CTA, Top/Standard/Blocktributes tabs, timeframe pills, and the main leaderboard table structure.
title: Build homepage leaderboard shell and navigation

acceptance_criteria: All approved categories render in the correct tab groupings; Bitmap is the baseline row in Top; tabs switch datasets without changing overall page structure.
close_reason: Implemented approved Bitmap and blocktribute category leaderboard datasets
description: Populate the leaderboard with the initial category set for v1: Bitmap, Patoshi, Billionaire, Bitmap Punk, Sub 100k, Epic Sat Block, Palindrome, Pizza Block, and Nakamoto Era. Wire the Top, Standard, and Blocktributes tabs to the correct subsets.
title: Populate leaderboard with initial Bitmap and blocktribute categories

acceptance_criteria: Recent Sales appears as a compact secondary section below the table; landing page remains minimal; browsing parcels is still available on /browse.
close_reason: Added recent sales section and preserved parcel browsing on /browse
description: Add the lightweight Recent Sales module below the leaderboard and make sure the existing browse experience is accessible from /browse rather than the homepage.
title: Add recent sales section and preserve browse route

description: Implement activity/page.tsx Server Component. Components: EventTypeFilter.tsx (pill tabs), ActivityTable.tsx (filterable table with canvas preview).
title: Phase 4: Activity feed page

description: Implement Navbar.tsx, StatsBar.tsx, FilterSidebar.tsx, BitmapCard.tsx, BrowseClient.tsx, RarityBadge, StatusPill, PriceDisplay, CountUp UI components. Also BitmapCanvas.tsx with PRNG and 5 renderers (city, grid, mondrian, punk, palindrome). App page.tsx Browse Server Component.
title: Phase 2: Browse page components

acceptance_criteria: Homepage uses a table-first layout; no left filter sidebar on the homepage; tabs are Top, Standard, and Blocktributes; rows include Bitmap plus the approved trait categories; no on-chain traits or floor 24h percent; recent sales module appears below the table; existing browse experience is available on /browse.
description: Replace the current parcel-browse homepage with a minimal market overview focused on Bitmap and blocktribute trait categories. The page should use a compact leaderboard layout with columns for Trait/Category, Floor, Volume, Sales, Listed, and Last 24h sparkline. Exclude floor change percentages and any on-chain leaderboard for the initial release. Keep the homepage focused on market summary, with the existing browse experience living on /browse.

description: Implement bitmap/[id]/page.tsx Server Component with 2-col layout. Components: MetadataPanel.tsx, ActionPanel.tsx, PriceHistoryChart.tsx (Recharts), RelatedBitmaps.tsx.
title: Phase 3: Detail page

close_reason: Already complete — Next.js 14 app bootstrapped, all dependencies installed, tailwind config, globals.css, lib files, and layout.tsx all exist and build passes.
description: Bootstrap Next.js 14 app (manually create package.json + config since create-next-app conflicts with existing files). Install recharts, lucide-react, clsx, tailwind-merge, swr. Set up tailwind.config.ts, globals.css, lib/types.ts, lib/mock-data.ts, lib/api.ts, lib/utils.ts, lib/bitmap-type.ts, and app/layout.tsx with Space Grotesk + Inter + JetBrains Mono fonts.
title: Phase 1: Bootstrap Next.js + foundation files

description: Implement analytics/page.tsx Server Component with Promise.all data fetches. Components: StatCard.tsx, FloorChart.tsx, VolumeChart.tsx, HolderDistribution.tsx, RarityDonut.tsx (all Recharts).
title: Phase 5: Analytics dashboard page

acceptance_criteria: Homepage, browse, detail, activity, analytics, and shared navigation/components have a coherent visual system; typography, spacing, borders, backgrounds, and interaction states are consistent; obvious visual rough edges are removed; key routes still build and lint cleanly.
description: Run a full frontend polish pass across the site so the homepage, browse flow, detail page, activity, analytics, navigation, and shared UI feel like one coherent Bitmap marketplace product. The visual language should align with the Bitmap/hooter monitor direction where appropriate: stronger hierarchy, cleaner spacing, coherent orange/yellow-on-black contrast, consistent table/card chrome, and reduced leftover prototype styling.
title: Polish website UI for a cohesive Bitmap marketplace experience
